title: Open question on $R_\ell(K_{1,2}, k)$ for odd $k$
statement_text: We do not know whether $R_\ell(K_{1,2}, k)$ is $k + 1$ or $k + 2$ when $k$ is odd.
context_text: For even $k$, the paper establishes $R_\ell(K_{1,2}, k) = k+1 = R(K_{1,2}, k)$ using the result of Häggkvist and Janssen that $\chi'_\ell(K_n) = \chi'(K_n)$ for odd $n$. The case of odd $k$ is equivalent to the List Colouring Conjecture for cliques of even order, which asks whether $\chi'_\ell(K_n)$ equals $n$ or $n-1$ for even $n$.
notes: Stated in prose; directly tied to the open case of the List Colouring Conjecture for cliques of even order.

title: Open problem on equality $R_\ell(K_r, 2) = R(K_r, 2)$ for $r > 3$
statement_text: For $r > 3$ we cannot even decide the equality of the two Ramsey numbers of $K_r$ when $k = 2$.
context_text: The classic probabilistic lower bound gives $2^{r/2} < R_\ell(K_r, 2) \leq R(K_r, 2) < 2^{2r}$. The equality $R_\ell(K_3, 2) = 6 = R(K_3, 2)$ is easily verified, but for larger complete graphs the question of whether the list and ordinary 2-colour Ramsey numbers coincide is entirely open.
notes: Stated in prose. The extracted text does not include Section 3 (the paper's dedicated open problems section), so further explicit problems posed there are not captured here.
